rationale: Both candidates independently surface the exact latent gap (the leftover get_cache_scale call in hunyuan_image3_transformer.py, later fixed in #4891) with specific file/line citations, and both correctly validate the core mapper-migration logic against the real upstream vLLM checkout, matching the human reviewer's core LGTM reasoning. X is more decisive and actionable — it flags the gap as a blocker with a concrete fix (apply the mapper pattern, add to _STALE_API_FILES) and stays anchored to PR-time tree state throughout, whereas Y downgrades the same finding to 'non-blocking' by reasoning from a later, unverifiable claim that it was 'since addressed on main' — a speculative post-hoc justification that muddles severity and is a riskier, harder-to-verify claim than X's grounded blocker. Y edges out slightly on recall (its test-coverage critique echoes the ground truth's real-checkpoint concern more closely than X's), but X's tighter, more assertive, and more verifiable findings give it the edge overall.
